channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Limits of AI Companions: Why Human Relationships Retain Meaning
item.description: As AI companions become more common, some people form emotional or even romantic attachments to them. Research indicates that about one in five high school students in the U.S. have experienced or kno...

item.title: IMF warns Middle East conflict may slow global economic growth and increase inflation
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has revised down its global growth forecast for 2026 to 3.1 per cent, from an earlier prediction of 3.3 per cent, due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. While the d...

item.title: Opposition Leaders Peter Obi, Atiku Abubakar and Rabiu Kwankwaso Attend ADC National Convention in Abuja Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Prominent opposition figures including former Labour Party presidential candidate Peter Obi, former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, and former Kano State Governor Rabiu Kwankwaso gathered at the Africa...

item.title: Hungary’s Election: The Implications of Viktor Orban's Defeat
item.description: In a historic election in Hungary, opposition leader Peter Magyar of the Tisza Party secured a resounding victory, defeating Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The election saw the highest voter turnout in ...

item.title: Lorry driver jailed for causing death of mother hit by crane equipment
item.description: A lorry driver, Kevin Miller, 71, from King's Lynn, Norfolk, has been sentenced to 13 months in prison after loose crane equipment on his vehicle struck 30-year-old Rebecca Ableman as she pushed her t...

item.title: JD Vance comments on disagreement between Trump and Pope Leo over US foreign policy
item.description: JD Vance, the Catholic vice-president of the United States, has spoken out in defence of former President Donald Trump following a spat with Pope Leo XIV regarding the Iran war. Pope Leo had criticise...

item.title: Desiree Ellis praises Banyana Banyana’s effort in 1-0 win over Algeria
item.description: Banyana Banyana coach Desiree Ellis expressed her satisfaction after her side secured a narrow 1-0 victory over Algeria in their first friendly match at Princess Magogo Stadium in Durban. Despite the ...

item.title: Analysis of Mark Carney's Political Strategy and Liberal Party Gains
item.description: In the wake of recent Liberal byelection victories, Prime Minister Mark Carney has secured a majority, causing significant concern for Conservative leader Pierre Poilievre. Carney's approach combines ...

item.title: Durban Shop Owners Affected by March and March Campaign in CBD
item.description: In Durban’s central business district, tensions have risen as the March and March movement conducted a campaign targeting foreign-owned shops, leading many to temporarily close. Somali shop owner Ahme...

item.title: UK Military Faces Savings Cuts Amid War Readiness Demands
item.description: The UK’s military top brass have been instructed to identify £3.5 billion in savings while simultaneously preparing for potential conflict, according to reports. Defence officials are under pressure t...

item.title: Skeleton of Infant Found Beneath Floorboards in Bishop Auckland Dates Back Centuries
item.description: Contractors renovating a building on Fore Bondgate in Bishop Auckland, County Durham, uncovered the skeleton of a baby boy, referred to in official documents as 'Baby Auckland'. The discovery, made in...

item.title: Harvey Weinstein Faces Retrial in New York for Third-Degree Rape Charge
item.description: Di retrial of Harvey Weinstein, di once-famous movie mogul, don start for Manhattan court, New York, on top rape charge wey jury no fit decide before. Weinstein dey accused of third-degree rape of act...

item.title: Student charged in fatal housemate stabbing case
item.description: The news story concerns a serious criminal case involving a university student who has allegedly stabbed a fellow housemate to death. Although the original article could not be accessed due to server ...

item.title: Uncertainty Surrounds Role of Nitish Kumar’s Son in New Bihar Government
item.description: As Samrat Choudhary of the BJP takes oath as Bihar's new Chief Minister, the political scene in the state is shifting. Nitish Kumar's son, Nishant Kumar, appears to be sidelined, with no immediate maj...

item.title: Authorities call for stronger standards to address skills shortage in Tanzania’s urban planning sector
item.description: Tanzania’s urban planning sector is facing a growing integrity challenge as unqualified individuals and firms continue to handle critical development projects, putting the quality and sustainability o...

item.title: AFP confirms repeated cyanide fishing in Ayungin Shoal despite China’s dismissal
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) have confirmed that cyanide fishing incidents in Ayungin Shoal, located in the West Philippine Sea, have been recurring. This follows multiple encounters and ...

item.title: Commission-Free Trading Heats Up Competition Among Canadian Online Brokerages
item.description: Andrew Galbraith, a new investing reporter at The Globe and Mail, explores the growing competition among Canada’s online brokerages as firms race to eliminate trading commissions and attract do-it-you...

item.title: SIU report uncovers widespread fraudulent marriages linked to immigration fraud in South Africa
item.description: The Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has uncovered a widespread network of fraudulent marriages and life partnerships in South Africa, used primarily to obtain Permanent Residence Permits (PRPs). The ...

item.title: El-Rufai Preparing for 2027 Presidential Election, Says Close Ally
item.description: A close ally of former Governor of Kaduna State, Nasir El-Rufai, has revealed that El-Rufai is planning to contest the 2027 presidential election. Hayatudeen Lawal Makarfi, a chieftain of the African ...

item.title: Virgin Voyages Diverts Bermuda Cruise to Saint John Due to Bad Weather
item.description: A planned Virgin Voyages cruise to Bermuda was rerouted to Saint John, New Brunswick, because of adverse weather conditions. The Valiant Lady, departing from New York on April 6, was redirected before...
